---
title: "Home"
---

::: {.hero}
# Ran Wang

::: {.subtitle}
Applied Empirical Research | Urban & Regional Economics |  
High-Skilled Mobility and Innovation
:::

::: {.lead-text}
I study how scientist mobility, knowledge networks, and urban innovation systems are related to the spatial organization of research and innovation. My work combines longitudinal multi-source data construction with spatial, network, and applied empirical methods, with a focus on high-skilled mobility, regional innovation, and measurement-validity audits for complex relational data.
:::

::: {.button-row}
[Research](research.qmd)
[Projects](projects.qmd)
[Download my CV](assets/cv/Ran_Wang_CV_Public.pdf){.btn .btn-primary}
[GitHub](https://github.com/Olivia-wr-research)
:::
:::

## Current focus

I work at the intersection of economic geography, urban and regional economics, science of science, and computational social science. I am particularly interested in how career trajectories, institutional platforms, collaboration relations, and city-level opportunity structures can be measured with defensible longitudinal data.

## Featured public project

[Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit](https://github.com/Olivia-wr-research/author-institution-city-linkage) is a public code repository that demonstrates synthetic author-institution-city linkage, schema validation, linkage and geocoding QA, duplicate and confidence diagnostics, and reproducible documentation. It does not redistribute real Scopus, OpenAlex, or restricted source data.
